content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments verified against actual code. Three are clean code quality improvements (dead parameter removal, documentation of legacy paths, improved error messages). The fourth identifies a genuine operational risk for in-flight legacy rows during upgrade. All four are well-founded and worth implementing.

## Votes

- `fr-20-2f99f9e1`: Accept
  Verified: both find_matching_running_task_run (model.rs:462) and matching_finalized_task_runs (model.rs:480) accept _started_at: DateTime<Utc> but never reference it. Matching is purely by run_id via entry.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id). Call sites at fs.rs:2816-2821 and fs.rs:2849-2854 pass started_at for no effect. Dead parameters in function signatures are a genuine maintenance hazard — they mislead callers and make the matching contract ambiguous. Removing the parameter from signatures and call sites is low-risk and improves clarity.
- `fr-20-33568ff5`: Accept
  Verified: find_collapse_group_index (model.rs:516-530) falls through to unique_open_legacy_group_index for entries with run_id: None, and legacy_group_accepts_completion (model.rs:564-573) matches terminal entries to open legacy starts by started_at. This is a read-only backward-compat path for collapsing persisted legacy data. If acceptance criteria state 'all runless matching/backfill code is removed,' the survival of this code without documentation will confuse every future reviewer. Option (a) — adding a comment block explaining this is deliberately retained for read-only collapse of pre-existing legacy data — is the correct approach since legacy data may exist on disk.
- `fr-20-6b16d921`: Accept
  Verified: in update_task_run (fs.rs:2962-3014), when no exact run_id match exists, open_matches are collected without run_id filtering. The single-match arm (line 2998-3005) helpfully includes the mismatched run_id in the error. But the multi-match arm (line 3007-3013) only says 'ambiguous task run update' without listing which run_ids are open. Adding the mismatched run_id(s) to the multi-match error is a trivial change with high diagnostic value — operators hitting this path need to know the competing run_ids to diagnose corrupt state.
- `fr-20-dfd9659f`: Accept
  Verified: find_matching_running_task_run requires entry.run_id.as_deref() == Some(run_id), making legacy rows with run_id: None invisible to the exact-match path in update_task_run. When a new start arrives for the same bead, fail_superseded_running_attempt (fs.rs:2702-2723) uses same_attempt matching which handles legacy rows via started_at fallback — marking the legitimate in-progress legacy row as Failed. This means a mid-flight milestone from a pre-upgrade build will be silently discarded as a superseded retry rather than completed. A migration step to assign run_ids to open legacy rows (or at minimum documenting the upgrade constraint) is needed to prevent data loss during version transitions.
